replies.author: @roman_abelardo
replies.text:
Antiguamente venían cables para bujías de automotor, llevaban una resistencia en el interior del capuchón que conectaba a la bujías. En teoría se empleaban para sacar las interferencias (sonido) del motor que se reproducían en Radio auto/stereos.
Hoy quedó en desuso para ese fin pero tengo entendido que lo mejoraron para obtener mayor chispa en vehículos de competencia.
PDT: Los antiguos cables tenían una vida ciertamente limitada, quizás por resecarse la envoltura/cobertura de PVC en el Interior del motor a combustible que provocaba pequeñas interrupciones...
Hoy seguramente, lleven en su interior un cable de cobre 🤔

author: @ianfisher6561
text: Potassium permanganate (condis crystal) was a very popular chemical back in the day. Most medicine cabinets had a little bottle of it. It was used in the public swimming pools foot baths. We used the glycerine and potassium permanganate reaction quite a lot. We found if you ground the potassium permanganate into a fine powder the reaction was much faster. Really enjoyed this posting brought back memories of a well rounded misguided youth.

author: @THR33SHOES46and2
text: Been awhile. Glad to see that you are still banging away on graphene experiments.  Your attention to your channel and our comments has always been somewhat impressive to me and i hope that this wont be some kind of abuse of that. I found something or someone that may be of some interest to you. This guy was a cotton farmer here in the wild wild west of the U.S. you were the 1st person i thought of when he expressed a theory of a substance perhaps falling into a 2 dimensional realm when it was cooling or heating. The response from some people is that its a bunch of woo woo. Thus guy spent a lot of his own time money and resources to get a patent on mono atomic noble elements. I am not a genius by any stretch of the imagination, in fact a lot of this, to me, is like showing a dog a card trick. Check out, David R. Hudson ormus conference enota, here on youtube. Like i said this stuff goes over my head so much it gives me atheletes scalp, but if it helps you to save the world in some small way then so did I. Curious to know what your opinion of this video is. Even if it means im an easily distracted spastic.

author: @dgpreston5593
text:
Have you seen his video that discloses a chlorine compound? Will try to locate it...

Still looking for the video, but my notes record CL2O8 written on a paper he is showing to an assistant or reporter.

replies.author: @Buzzhumma
replies.text: Yes I heard him say a little bit of chlorine or chloride  in regards to the fumes. He said it in Russian and it was translated to English text

replies.author: @dgpreston5593
replies.text: @Buzzhumma seems like he was intercalating with perchlorate and the explosive exfoliation was triggered by a clear solution applied to the ceramic tray onto which his treated graphite pellets were placed.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: he has a pent in which he discloses the material and a source he says he got it from - if you look up the source it is a defunct address for a company owned by his brother. With Petrik you have to entertain the possibility he may not be telling quite all of the truth. It's clearly and exfoliation reaction and that must be based on a catalysed heat based explosive intercalant. there aren't many of those around.
